response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. ***

Reach for the discount, and the customer will pay less for each item. The handcrafted table, originally priced at $200, will be reduced by 15%, which means the customer pays 85% of $200. This results in a discounted price of $170. The factory-made chairs, originally priced at $150, will also be reduced by 15%, resulting in a discounted price of $127.50. The total amount the customer pays for both items is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. ***

The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items, which means the customer will pay less for each item. The handcrafted table, originally priced at $200, will be reduced by 15%, which means the customer pays 85% of $200. This results in a discounted price of $170. The factory-made chairs, originally priced at $150, will also be reduced by 15%, resulting in a discounted price of $127.50. The total amount the customer pays for both items is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. ***
